Excellent! Now I have all the information. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary:

---

## Summary of Findings

### 1. Entity Model File Location and Classes

**File Path:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Engineering\Models\PartNumber.cs`

**Three entity classes defined (lines 456-486):**

```csharp
//===============================================================
// CLASS START : PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI
//===============================================================

[Table("PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI")]
public class tbl_PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI : BaseEntity
{
  [Key]
  [ScaffoldColumn(false)]
  [Display(Name = "AUTOID")]
  public int AUTOID { get; set; }
  [Display(Name = "PARTID")]
  public int? PARTID { get; set; }
  [Display(Name = "AREAID")]
  public int? AREAID { get; set; }
  [Display(Name = "SIDE")]
  public string SIDE { get; set; }
  public virtual IEnumerable<SelectListItem> FacilityList { get; set; }
}

public class tbl_PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI_FULL : tbl_PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI
{
  [Display(Name = "Facility")]
  public string Facility_Name { get; set; }

  [Display(Name = "Area")]
  public string Area { get; set; }

}

public class tbl_PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI_FULL_partial : tbl_PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI
{
  public IQueryable<tbl_PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_MERGE_AUTO_AT_AOI_FULL> Items { get; set; }

  public int id { get; set; }
}

//===============================================================
// CLASS START : EN_idt_stencils
```

---

### 2. Database Project Files

#### a. .sqlproj File Path
**File Path:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Database_MES_Database\MES\MES.sqlproj`

#### b. How Table .sql Files Are Referenced in the .sqlproj

**Pattern Used:** `<Build Include="dbo\Tables\[TableName].sql" />`

**Example Entries (3 samples):**
```xml
<Build Include="dbo\Tables\PART_UNITS_ARRAY.sql" />
<Build Include="dbo\Tables\PART_UNITS_LOG.sql" />
<Build Include="dbo\Tables\PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_ARRAY_INDEX.sql" />
```

Additional examples:
```xml
<Build Include="dbo\Tables\PART_OPERATION.sql" />
<Build Include="dbo\Tables\PART_OPERATION_AREAS.sql" />
<Build Include="dbo\Tables\PART_OPERATION_AREAS_TCP.sql" />
```

All table files follow this structure within `<ItemGroup>` elements containing `<Build>` directives.

#### c. Template SQL File: PART_OPERATION_AREAS_TCP.sql

**File Path:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Database_MES_Database\MES\dbo\Tables\PART_OPERATION_AREAS_TCP.sql`

**Full Content:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE [dbo].[PART_OPERATION_AREAS_TCP]
(
	[AutoID]       INT           IDENTITY (1, 1) NOT NULL,
    [PartID]       INT           NOT NULL,
    [AreaID]       INT           NOT NULL,
    [Side]         NVARCHAR (3)  NOT NULL,
    [IP]           NVARCHAR (20) NULL,
    [Port]         NVARCHAR (8)  NULL,
    [Timer]        INT           NULL,
    [Facility_Key] INT           NULL,
    [Part_Key]     INT           NOT NULL, 
    [CLIENT_OR_SERVER] VARCHAR(30) NULL, 
    [TCP_TYPE] VARCHAR(30) NULL, 
    [SLAVE] INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, 
    CONSTRAINT [PK_PART_OPERATION_AREAS_TCP] PRIMARY KEY ([PartID], [AreaID], [Side], [Part_Key])
)
```

---

All file paths are absolute and verified through the repo structure.
